text:
Interest payments already consume 25% of all government revenue. 

If the massive deficit spending continues, there will only be money for interest payments and nothing else! No social security, no medical, no defense … nothing.
embedded_text:
Our annual US budget deficit is going to be over $2 trillion per year going forward.

We pay over $100 billion per month in interest on the national debt. $1.2 trillion per year. That is about 25% of all government revenue going to pay interest on the debt. https://t.co/tvAqKefb0Q
Let’s flatten the curve https://t.co/IjsoGaheTw

This is for the first 7 months and the fiscal year ends in September. The latest available data is as of April. The already accrued deficit amounts to over $1 trillion: $1,049 billion. text:
Early Atlas rockets used stainless, but it was certainly (incorrectly) considered to be far inferior to composites. 

All things considered, including strain-hardened strength at cryo, toughness, ease of welding on attachments, no need for paint and resistance to high temperatures during atmospheric entry, steel beats composites for Starship. 

And it costs 2% as much.

embedded_text:
Remember when San Francisco couldn’t do anything about the crime everywhere and then cleaned it up overnight for Xi’s visit?

Turns out the solution to the Problem of Evil government is that simple. 

They are aware.
They’re not impotent.
They’re just evil.

text:
This tweet 9 years ago was how I started the Autopilot, now AI, team at Tesla.

Ashok, who now leads the team, was the first person I interviewed.

Milan, who leads Optimus, also joined very early. 

Many of the key people in Tesla AI have been there from the beginning.

text:
Bannon is such a retarded liar. Dumber than a doorstop. 

SpaceX revenue this year will be $15.5B and NASA is only $1.1B. 

For that NASA revenue, SpaceX does all astronaut transport to the Space Station at roughly half the cost of Boeing, who thus far has failed to complete a single astronaut back and had to be rescued by SpaceX! It wasn’t some “gift”. 

If Bannon’s dumb as fuck reasoning made any sense, then anyone who bought products from any company would automatically own that company. 

Bannon is truly peak retard.

Historical context reveals SpaceX's Raptor engines, powering Super Heavy, use liquid methane and oxygen, producing ~250-300 tons of thrust per engine across 33 units, yet a 2023 study in *Energy Policy* suggests rocket energy calculations often ignore conversion losses, reducing the realistic contribution to under 0.5% of global energy. - embedded_text:
Western Civilization has largely been a force for good.

A prime example is the British Empire. In many places it occupied it:

-raised the standard of living
-developed infrastructure
-promoted education

It also single-handedly ended slavery for much of the world…🧵 https://t.co/EvVrMJ9d4q

text:
This goes beyond a question of money. Even if there was infinite money, there would not be infinite doctors. 

The number of doctors in California, for example, is fairly constant, so if the number of patients increase significantly, there will not be enough doctors to take care of them all.

This will manifest in very long wait time for medical care, even for emergencies.

Actually, humanity is only about 1% of the way towards a Type I civilization.

We could generate at least 2 orders of magnitude more electricity than we do now.

You can tell immediately that someone is a massive liar if they’re claiming I’m on drugs, as I am one of the most photographed people on Earth and am in meetings with a wide array of people 7 days a week!

Moreover, I was randomly drug tested for ~3 years after one semi-puff on @JoeRogan. Not the slightest trace of drugs or alcohol was found.

If you’re interested in applying advanced AI to the 𝕏 content recommendation system, join us! 

Getting this right is fundamental to improving the collective intelligence of humanity.

text: Legacy media is one big psy op
embedded_text:
Here’s a list of all the news networks who have not covered Cory Booker’s salute:

- NYTimes
- CNN
- Washington Post
- MSNBC
- NPR
- USA Today
- Reuters
- Axios
- ABC News

Every single one of them wrote stories on Elon Musk’s “salute”…

…do you get it yet? https://t.co/s4VuquhbEs

text: Great work turning a lengthy paperwork retirement process into a simple digital one!
embedded_text:
On June 2, online digital retirement became the government standard at https://t.co/oYBNuZ7Xov!  Great teamwork by OPM and DOGE. 

Next up (end of July): getting the first retirement benefit payments to retirees faster (currently takes multiple months).  Will report back in July!

embedded_text:
I'm all in for more cuts to the bill. It's big, but it's not beautiful yet.

The Senate's job is to make the bill better before sending it back to us. The House's job is to pass DOGE rescissions and EO codification bills that are collecting dust.

Not to go home. https://t.co/HakdygclMo

text:
Whatever. 

Keep the EV/solar incentive cuts in the bill, even though no oil & gas subsidies are touched (very unfair!!), but ditch the MOUNTAIN of DISGUSTING PORK in the bill. 

In the entire history of civilization, there has never been legislation that both big and beautiful. Everyone knows this!

Either you get a big and ugly bill or a slim and beautiful bill. 

Slim and beautiful is the way. 

 https://t.co/RdlmTmoKej
